title: Boat Rental in Djerba: What to Know Before You Go (2026)
html:
Exploring Djerba by boat offers a unique perspective on Tunisia's largest island, with departures from Houmt Souk Marina. A half-day motorboat hire typically costs from €150, and you can compare verified listings on BoatHire24 to find the perfect vessel for your trip.

Where to set off in Djerba

Djerba's main boating hubs are Houmt Souk, Ajim, and El Kantara, each offering different departure points and facilities. Houmt Souk Marina is the largest, with berths for over 200 boats and easy access to the town's souk and restaurants. Ajim port, on the southern coast, is a quieter launch point for exploring the Bou Grara lagoon. El Kantara, linked to the mainland by a causeway, provides quick access to the eastern coast and nearby islets. For those venturing further, the harbour at Zarzis, a short sail east, is a gateway to the Kerkennah Islands. One option worth a look is the Salty Sun Charters for hire.

Which boat to rent

Your choice depends on group size, budget, and whether you prefer speed or leisure.

  1. Small motorboat (4, 6 guests), ideal for coastal exploration and fishing trips, from €150 for a half-day.
  2. RIB (Rigid Inflatable Boat) (6, 8 guests), fast and nimble for reaching remote coves, from €250 for a full day.
  3. Sailing catamaran (8, 12 guests), spacious and stable for day charters with a crew, from €500 for a full day.
  4. Crewed motor yacht (10, 16 guests), luxury experience with onboard amenities, such as the Salty Sun Charters for hire at €1,200 per day.
  5. Traditional fishing boat (4, 8 guests), authentic local style, often with a skipper who knows the best fishing spots, from €200 for a half-day.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Djerba?

Rental prices in Djerba range from around €50 per person for a shared tour to over €1,500 per day for a crewed luxury yacht. A typical shared island-hopping tour costs €80, €120 per person. For a private motorboat, expect to pay €150, €400 for a half-day. A full-day catamaran charter for a group of ten often falls between €700 and €1,000. Crewed yachts with a skipper and provisions start at €1,200. These rates reflect the high season; discounts are common in spring and autumn. You can hire a boat through BoatHire24 to compare the latest prices and availability.

  • Shared tour: from €50 per person
  • Small motorboat half-day: from €150
  • Catamaran full-day: from €500
  • Crewed yacht: from €1,200 per day

Do you need a licence to rent a boat in Djerba?

For most small motorboats and sailing vessels, no licence is required if you hire with a skipper; bareboat charters may require proof of competency. Tunisian regulation generally requires a licence for boats over 15 kW (20 hp) unless a licensed skipper is on board. BoatHire24 operators always confirm the exact requirements for each listing. The high season runs from June to September, when the prevailing north-westerly wind, the Gregale, can pick up in the afternoon. Learn to read the weather and keep an eye on forecasts before setting out.

A useful local tip: anchor near the sandbar off the coast of El Kantara for calm swimming and excellent snorkelling, but avoid the area during strong Gregale winds when the current becomes unpredictable.

At a glance

  • Price range: €50, €1,500 per day
  • Peak months: June to September
  • Trip length: 2 hours to full day
  • Max capacity: 2, 16 guests
  • Currency: EUR (prices often quoted in euros; local dinars accepted on board)

Questions about boat rental in Djerba

Can I rent a boat without a skipper in Djerba?

Yes, if you hold a recognised boating licence and the operator verifies it, you can take a bareboat charter.

What is the best month for boating in Djerba?

May and October offer warm weather, fewer crowds, and lower prices than the peak summer months.

Are there day trips to the Kerkennah Islands from Djerba?

Yes, several operators run day trips from Djerba and nearby Zarzis, covering the 18 nautical mile crossing in about one hour.

What should I bring on a boat trip in Djerba?

Pack sunscreen, a hat, swimwear, a towel, and snacks; most charter boats provide water and soft drinks.

A practical way to browse all available options in Djerba is to use the boat search on BoatHire24, which lets you filter by boat type, price, and guest capacity.
